text: Robotic Diverting Colostomy Patient ML is a thirty eight year old female with grade four sacral decubitus ulcer. Past medical history is significant for encephalopathy and morbid obesity. Past surgical history is significant for an open cholecystectomy and an open appendectomy.

The surgical team employs a four trocar technique, inserting a 12mm trocar for the camera at the umbilicus, an 8mm trocar in the right upper quadrant, and another 12mm trocar in the right lower quadrant for the stapler and robotic cannula. An additional 12mm trocar is placed at the preoperatively marked ostomy site. A 5mm trocar is used to clear extensive omental adhesions obscuring the right side of the abdomen. The robotic system is docked over the patient's left lateral abdomen.

The surgeon performs lateral to medial mobilization of the sigmoid and descending colon, continuing cephalad dissection to the splenic flexure. The inferior mesenteric artery is ligated using a vessel sealer, with ligation extending to the level of the rectum. The colon's mobilization is confirmed before proceeding with the rectum transection.

The surgeon uses a grasper to secure the proximal colon through the twelve millimeter trocar site. A Proline suture is applied to mark the rectum for future colostomy reversal. The proximal colon is exteriorized through the trocar site, and the ostomy is matured.
